pi_items.source: Task description (task.json line 5) and export_result.sh line 10
setup_task.sh line 50 (path.name = "FencePath"), verified in export_result.sh line 58
setup_task.sh line 32 (post.name = "FencePost"), verified in export_result.sh line 59
Task description (task.json line 5): 'Resample the curve to have points exactly every 1.5 meters.' Confirmed in export_result.sh lines 79-82 analysis comment: '20m / 1.5m spacing ~= 13-14 posts'

Curve geometry analysis: S-curve with 4 vertices at (0,0,0), (5,5,0), (10,0,0), (15,5,0) yields ~21.2m total length. With 1.5m spacing: 21.2/1.5 = 14.1 expected instances. Minimum of 10 is conservative. Confirmed in export_result.sh line 81: 'if 10 <= instance_count <= 20'

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task that generates a Blender scene programmatically. The setup script creates an S-shaped Bezier curve named 'FencePath' (approximately 21.2 meters long) and a 'FencePost' object. The expected output is a Blender file saved to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/procedural_fence.blend' with a procedural Geometry Nodes modifier that instances the FencePost object at 1.5-meter intervals along the curve, yielding approximately 14 instances. The minimum expected instance count is 10.
